fields.description:
## Why
On failure the bot must NEVER land a partial/inconsistent stack and NEVER evict a change from within a stack (that would orphan its descendants). The whole stack returns to its owning agent to remediate ‚Äî this is the one Goal-2 exception (agent pulled in only when code must change).

## What
Handle the two failure modes in the auto-lander loop: CI-fail (post-rebase `Verified -1`) and rebase-conflict (`POST /rebase` `409`). The recorded outcome is the single source of truth consumed by the agent contract.

## Dependencies
Depends on **S2** (`perovskite-frilly-grebe`, 0ee8-08de-bf0f-45ec) ‚Äî the core loop: this story adds the failure/edge branches to S2's loop and consumes its `wipChain` + stack-selection, so the happy-path loop must exist first.

## Outcome record
**Gerrit is the source of truth; the persisted store is a reconstruct-from-Gerrit cache, not an authoritative record.** The bot persists ONLY `needs_rebase` markers in the auto-lander state dir (project-infra state, NOT a rebar event), keyed by `change_id + patchset_SHA` and **self-invalidating**: a marker whose SHA no longer matches the change's current patchset in Gerrit is IGNORED (a new patchset means the rebase was resolved). `merged` / `ci_failed` / `review_failed` are NOT persisted ‚Äî they are derived LIVE from Gerrit at read time (S4 already does this). Marker shape: `{change_id, patchset_SHA, stack_id, change_ids[], failing_change_id?, tested_sha, recorded_at, acknowledged}`. A re-run overwrites the prior marker for the same `change_id + patchset_SHA`. `stack_id` = the Gerrit topic the auto-lander assigns to a stack at selection time (falling back to the tip change-id for a single-change stack); it is the shared key across S2/S3/S4/S9. `acknowledged`: the writer sets `acknowledged=false` when recording and `true` after `Autosubmit`-removal for every change in the stack completes; on restart, any `acknowledged=false` marker re-drives the removal to completion (idempotent). A rolling per-transition **log line** is kept for debugging even though the authoritative record is single-slot. S4 (land command) and S9 (batch failure) consume this cache + live Gerrit state as the outcome.

## Scope
Failure/edge paths of the core-loop service; the recorded outcome feeds the agent contract story.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **CI-flake auto-recheck (before hand-back):** on a post-rebase `Verified -1`, do a **bounded auto-`recheck`** ‚Äî 1 retry on the SAME patchset ‚Äî BEFORE recording `ci_failed` and stripping `Autosubmit`; only hand the stack back if it fails again. (The `gerrit-verify` workflow casts a single **undifferentiated** `Verified -1` for all failure modes ‚Äî there is NO machine-readable infra-vs-test signal in the vote/label/REST API to branch on ‚Äî so the auto-`recheck` applies to ANY post-rebase `-1`; the single bounded retry is itself what absorbs a transient flake, and a real test-failure that reproduces on the recheck correctly hands back.) **Recheck trigger + state machine (concrete):** the bot triggers CI re-execution by **posting the exact Gerrit comment `recheck`** ‚Äî which the gerrit-to-platform ChatOps maps to the `verify` workflow (`gerrit_to_platform.ini.template`: `recheck = verify`), re-running `gerrit-verify.yaml` (the bot posts comments as its own Gerrit user; no extra grant needed). It then enters a **`rechecking` in-flight sub-state** (an S3-owned flag on the `wipChain`, NOT a new S2 phase) and reads the vote sequence correctly: `gerrit-verify.yaml`'s `clear-vote` job first **resets `Verified‚Üí0`** at run start (GerriScary-safe) ‚Äî the bot MUST treat this transient `0` as *recheck-running*, NOT as "no result," and MUST NOT re-post `recheck`; it then waits for the terminal vote ‚Äî `+1` ‚Üí clear the flag and proceed to submit; `-1` ‚Üí record `ci_failed` and hand back. The recheck is bounded to **one** post per patchset (the flag prevents a re-post loop). A single flake must not poison an otherwise-green stack (the epic's "zero babysitting" goal).
- [ ] CI-fail: after the auto-recheck still shows `Verified -1`, record a `ci_failed` outcome (derived live from Gerrit), then remove `Autosubmit` from **all changes in the affected stack** (not just the failing change), and stop driving the stack; post NO bot comment (the native `-1` + run logs are the signal ‚Äî no redundant channel).
- [ ] **Persisted state is a reconstruct-from-Gerrit cache:** persist ONLY `needs_rebase` markers keyed by `change_id + patchset_SHA`; a persisted marker is IGNORED if its patchset SHA no longer matches the change's current patchset in Gerrit. `merged`/`ci_failed`/`review_failed` are NEVER persisted ‚Äî they are derived live from Gerrit at read time. Keep a rolling per-transition log line for debugging.
- [ ] Rebase-conflict: on `POST /rebase` `409`, record a `needs_rebase` outcome event-driven on that change/stack (NOT a continuously-maintained up-to-date vote ‚Äî that would churn O(open) per landing), then remove `Autosubmit` from all changes in the stack.
- [ ] **Stack ‚Üí owner:** the owning agent is the stack's uploader/owner (`owner._account_id` on the tip change); "hand back" = record ONE outcome for the stack + remove `Autosubmit` from every change in it. Never partial-land; never evict a change from within a stack.
- [ ] Never `allow_conflicts` ‚Äî a `409` from `POST /rebase` routes to the `needs_rebase` path; no conflict-marker patchset is ever created or submitted.
- [ ] **Idempotent ordering under retries/restart:** the outcome is recorded **before** `Autosubmit` removal; label-removal treats `404`/`409` as a no-op; the `stack_id` upsert key makes re-runs overwrite rather than duplicate; on restart an unacknowledged record re-drives the label removal to completion.
